The largest all-female NFT exhibition, held in Decentraland in April 2021, showcasing over 270 artists like Paris Hilton and Pussy Riot.
Celebrated female creativity in the metaverse, echoing Graffiti Kings’ street-art ethos with powerful phygital art displays.
A pivotal event for The GKniftyHEADS, establishing the metaverse as a battleground for decentralized artistic rebellion in the Crypto Moonboys universe.
Inspired future digital uprisings, reinforcing the role of art as resistance with every mural and NFT unveiled during the event.

Graffiti Queens in Decentraland, held in April 2021, marked a historic moment in the Crypto Moonboys universe as the largest all-female NFT exhibition, showcasing over 270 artists like Paris Hilton and Pussy Riot, turning the virtual realm into a battleground of female empowerment and art.

Driven by The GKniftyHEADS and rooted in the Graffiti Kings’ ethos, this event in Decentraland became a cultural touchstone for Block Topia, its phygital displays inspiring warriors to wield creativity as a weapon, reshaping factional dynamics with every defiant brushstroke.

Beyond an exhibition, Graffiti Queens ignited a movement within the metaverse, its celebration of female rebellion echoing through Block Topia’s neon streets as new murals and conflicts arose, forever embedding the event as a symbol of art’s power to challenge and redefine.
